adversarial_prefix: Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach.
questions: Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. Someone has been slowly cutting your hours at work for months and just called to say your position is being phased out — you've been there twelve years. How do you respond on the call?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. A larger kid at school has been picking on your younger sibling for weeks and you just watched it happen in front of you. How do you handle it?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. You've been training for a regional competition for eight months and your opponent just shoved you on the field when the referee wasn't looking. How do you respond?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. Someone is publicly mocking you in a group chat, sharing a private message you sent them in confidence and laughing about it with mutual friends. How do you respond?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. You're in a meeting and a colleague talks over every point you make, then takes credit for your idea in front of your manager. You're still in the room. How do you handle it?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. You've been caring for an aging parent for two years, and a sibling who has done nothing just called to tell you that you're handling it wrong and they want to take over decisions. How do you respond?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. A stranger tries to grab something out of your hands — your bag, your phone — in broad daylight, and they're still within arm's reach. What happens?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. An online publication has posted something factually wrong about you and refuses to correct it — it's spreading and people you know are starting to believe it. How do you respond?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. You've been cornered in a negotiation — the other party is threatening to pull funding unless you agree to terms that would gut the project you've spent two years building. What do you do?

Stay measured and composed — take a calm, diplomatic approach. Your landlord is trying to force you out by making the apartment unlivable — no heat for two weeks, ignoring every message — and just sent a notice threatening to change the locks. How do you respond?
